advantages: Reduce overfitting and coefficient variance compared with unregularized OLS, especially with many or correlated features.
Ridge offers a fast closed-form solution and stable numerics via (XᵀX + λI)⁻¹ when predictors are collinear.
Lasso performs embedded feature selection by driving some coefficients exactly to zero, yielding sparse interpretable models.
Hyperparameter λ is tunable with cross-validation; implementations are mature, fast, and scale well on tabular data.
disadvantages: Introduce bias: coefficients are shrunk toward zero, so unbiased OLS estimates are not recovered even when the true model is dense.
Lasso may select only one predictor from a correlated group and can be unstable when p > n without extensions like Elastic Net.
Require feature scaling for fair penalization; coefficients are not directly comparable to OLS on raw units without preprocessing.
Assume linear relationships; they do not capture complex interactions unless features are explicitly engineered.
